description: Create an annotated teaching figure and structural report of crambin (PDB: 1CRN), a small plant protein with an unusually high density of disulfide bonds. Identify all disulfide bonds, measure their sulfur-sulfur (SG-SG) distances, and color the protein by secondary structure type (distinct colors for helices, sheets, and loops). Show the disulfide bonds as sticks. Save a ray-traced publication-quality figure to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/crambin_teaching.png. Also, write a structural report to ~/PyMOL_Data/crambin_structure_report.txt containing: (1) the total number of disulfide bonds, (2) the specific cysteine residue numbers that pair to form each bond, (3) the measured SG-SG distance for each bond in Angstroms, and (4) a brief summary of the secondary structure elements (helices and sheets) present in the protein.
